exercises.title: Trace HttpRequest builder chain
exercises.scenario: HttpRequest is built with a fluent builder.

exercises.title: Build a GET request with Bearer auth
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blank to add a Bearer token authorization header.

blanks.hint: The standard prefix for JWT token auth

hints: This is the most common REST API authentication scheme

exercises.title: Parse mock HTTP response with Jackson
exercises.scenario: Simulate what response.body() returns and parse it.
exercises.instructions:
Given a simulated response body string (as would come from response.body()), parse it with Jackson and print product details.

Expected output:
Product: Wireless Mouse
Price: ₹799
In stock: true

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Product: Wireless Mouse
Price: ₹799
In stock: true

hints: In real code: String body = response.body();

exercises.title: Build POST request with JSON body
exercises.scenario: POST request with Content-Type and JSON body.
exercises.instructions:
Build a POST HttpRequest for creating an order. Print the method, URI, Content-Type header, and body.

Expected output:
Method: POST
URI: https://api.example.com/v1/orders
Content-Type: application/json
Body present: true

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Method: POST
URI: https://api.example.com/v1/orders
Content-Type: application/json
Body present: true

hints: Content-Type must be set manually for JSON

exercises.title: Status code handling with switch
exercises.instructions:
Write a `handleResponse(int statusCode, String body)` method using switch. Test with 200, 201, 404, 500.

Expected output:
200: OK — parsing body
201: Created — new resource id extracted
404: Not found — returning empty
500: Server error — retry later
exercises.starter_code: // handleResponse: switch on HTTP status code

test_cases.expected_stdout:
200: OK — parsing body
201: Created — new resource id extracted
404: Not found — returning empty
500: Server error — retry later

hints: case 500, 502, 503 -> multiple values in one case

exercises.title: Build Basic Auth header
exercises.scenario: Basic auth encodes username:password in base64.
exercises.instructions:
Build an HttpRequest with Basic auth (username:password base64 encoded). Print the Authorization header value.

Expected output:
Authorization: Basic cmF6b3JwYXk6c2VjcmV0MTIz

exercises.title: Simulate and parse GET response
exercises.scenario: Full GET → parse → display pipeline (simulated response).
exercises.instructions:
Simulate an HTTP GET for stock quotes. For each symbol, parse the simulated JSON response and print a formatted dashboard.

Expected output:
=== Stock Dashboard ===
TCS          ₹3,890  +4.1%
RELIANCE     ₹2,450  +1.2%
INFY         ₹1,560  -3.8%
hints: MAPPER is a static singleton — not created per call

exercises.title: Build POST request with Jackson body
exercises.scenario: Serialize request body with Jackson before sending.
exercises.instructions:
Build a POST request to create an order. Use Jackson to serialize the OrderRequest POJO as the request body. Print the request details.

Expected output:
Method: POST
Content-Type: application/json
Body: {"productId":"P001","quantity":2,"unitPrice":799.0}
hints: BodyPublishers.ofString(jsonBody) wraps the string as body
OrderRequest needs public fields for Jackson serialization

exercises.title: Simulate REST CRUD operations
exercises.scenario: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE — all four HTTP methods.
exercises.instructions:
Simulate four REST operations with mock responses. Show what each operation does and its simulated status code.

Expected output:
GET    /api/products/P001 → 200 Found: Wireless Mouse
POST   /api/products       → 201 Created: P099
PUT    /api/products/P001 → 200 Updated: new price ₹699
DELETE /api/products/P001 → 204 Deleted

test_cases.expected_stdout:
GET    /api/products/P001 → 200 Found: Wireless Mouse
POST   /api/products       → 201 Created: P099
PUT    /api/products/P001 → 200 Updated: new price ₹699
DELETE /api/products/P001 → 204 Deleted

hints: 204 No Content is standard for DELETE
201 Created is standard for successful POST

exercises.title: Response header parsing
exercises.scenario: HTTP response headers provide metadata about the response.
hints: .map(Integer::parseInt) transforms the string to int inside Optional

exercises.title: API client class with error handling
exercises.scenario: Encapsulate HTTP operations in a client class.
exercises.instructions:
Create `ProductApiClient` with:
- Singleton HttpClient and ObjectMapper
- `getProduct(String id)` → Optional<Product> (empty on 404)
- Handles 404 gracefully, other errors logged

Test with simulated responses.

Expected output:
Found P001: Wireless Mouse ₹799
P999: not found
Fetch P002 error: HTTP 500

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Found P001: Wireless Mouse ₹799
P999: not found

hints: ifPresentOrElse handles both present and absent cases
In real code: check response.statusCode() before parsing

exercises.title: Build URL with query parameters
exercises.scenario: Query parameters are part of the URI string.
exercises.instructions:
Build GET requests with query parameters for a product search API. Show two searches: by category and by price range.

Expected output:
Search 1: GET /api/products?category=Electronics&limit=10
Search 2: GET /api/products?minPrice=500&maxPrice=2000&sortBy=price
hints: Query params go in the URL: ?key=value&key2=value2
URI.create() accepts the full URL including query string
uri.getRawPath() = path only; uri.getRawQuery() = query string only

exercises.title: Retry on rate limit (429 response)
exercises.scenario: Rate limiting (429) requires waiting and retrying.
exercises.instructions:
Implement `fetchWithRetry(String url, int maxRetries)` that retries on simulated 429 responses (rate limited). Wait 100ms between retries. Give up after maxRetries.

Expected output:
Attempt 1: 429 Rate limited, waiting 100ms...
Attempt 2: 429 Rate limited, waiting 100ms...
Attempt 3: 200 OK — got response
Fetch succeeded after 3 attempts
exercises.starter_code: // Retry logic for rate-limited HTTP responses

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Attempt 1: 429 Rate limited, waiting 100ms...
Attempt 2: 429 Rate limited, waiting 100ms...
Attempt 3: 200 OK — got response
Fetch succeeded after 3 attempts

hints: Loop up to maxRetries; break on 200; sleep on 429
In real code: response.headers().firstValue('Retry-After') for wait time

exercises.title: Weather API client — parse nested JSON
exercises.scenario: Parse deeply nested JSON response from weather API.
exercises.instructions:
Simulate a weather API response. Parse: city name, temperature (from nested main.temp), weather description (from weather[0].description), humidity.

Expected output:
City: Hyderabad
Temp: 32°C
Condition: partly cloudy
Humidity: 65%
exercises.starter_code:
import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.*;
// Parse nested weather API JSON

test_cases.expected_stdout:
City: Hyderabad
Temp: 32°C
Condition: partly cloudy
Humidity: 65%

hints: path() is null-safe; get() can return null for arrays

exercises.title: Payment gateway client — full POST/GET cycle
exercises.scenario: Complete API integration: POST to create, GET to verify.
exercises.instructions:
Simulate a Razorpay-style payment flow:
1. POST to create payment order (returns orderId)
2. GET to fetch order status (using orderId)
3. Parse and display full payment details

Expected output:
Creating order for ₹1500...
Order created: ord_ABC123
Fetching status for ord_ABC123...
Payment Status: CREATED
Amount: ₹1500
Customer: Aisha (aisha@example.com)
Valid until: 2024-03-15T11:00:00

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Creating order for ₹1500...
Order created: ord_ABC123
Fetching status for ord_ABC123...
Payment Status: CREATED
Amount: ₹1500
Customer: Aisha (aisha@example.com)
Valid until: 2024-03-15T11:00:00

hints: POST response → parse orderId → use in GET request URL
In real code: replace simulate* with actual HTTP calls
